Japanese thoroughbred racehorse

**Eishin Flash** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): エイシンフラッシュ, foaled March 27, 2007) is a retired Japanese thoroughbred racehorse.[[1]](#cite_note-jbis-1)

His major wins include the 2010 [Tōkyō Yūshun](/wiki/T%C5%8Dky%C5%8D_Y%C5%ABshun "Tōkyō Yūshun"), the 2012 Autumn [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho"), the 2010 [Keisei Hai](/wiki/Keisei_Hai "Keisei Hai") and the 2013 [Mainichi Okan](/wiki/Mainichi_Okan "Mainichi Okan").

## Racing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Racing career")]

### 2009: Two-year old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 2009: Two-year old season")]

Eishin Flash debuted at a debut race for 2 year olds over 1800 meters (turf) on July 12 at [Hanshin](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse"), finishing in 6th place with [Yuichi Fukunaga](/wiki/Yuichi_Fukunaga "Yuichi Fukunaga") in the saddle. Two months later, the horse had his first win at another maiden race held at Kyoto, this time with [Hiroyuki Uchida](/w/index.php?title=Hiroyuki_Uchida&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hiroyuki Uchida (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%86%85%E7%94%B0%E5%8D%9A%E5%B9%B8 "ja:内田博幸")] as his jockey. Fukunaga once again became the horse's jockey later at the Hagi Stakes but finished 3rd. Later on, with Uchida back as jockey, Eishin Flash won the [Erika Sho](/w/index.php?title=Erika_Sho&action=edit&redlink=1 "Erika Sho (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%82%A8%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AB%E8%B3%9E "ja:エリカ賞")], leading to Uchida becoming the main jockey.[[5]](#cite_note-raceform-5)

### 2010: Three-year old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2010: Three-year old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Eishin_Flash.jpg/250px-Eishin_Flash.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eishin_Flash.jpg)

Eishin Flash at the Tokyo Yushun

At the [Keisei Hai](/wiki/Keisei_Hai "Keisei Hai"), because Uchida was injured in another race, [Norihiro Yokoyama](/wiki/Norihiro_Yokoyama "Norihiro Yokoyama") briefly took over the role of jockey. Eishin Flash was nonetheless the most favored to win owing to the Erika Sho victory, and subsequently won the race, making it his first graded race victory. Later, the horse was scheduled to run the [Wakaba Stakes](/w/index.php?title=Wakaba_Stakes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wakaba Stakes (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%8B%A5%E8%91%89%E3%82%B9%E3%83%86%E3%83%BC%E3%82%AF%E3%82%B9 "ja:若葉ステークス")] but was forced to skip the race as the horse contracted pneumonia.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The horse was then sent to the [Satsuki Shō](/wiki/Satsuki_Sh%C5%8D "Satsuki Shō"), where he was the 11th most favored to win but managed to finish 3rd, securing priority entry to the Japanese Derby.

At the 77th [Tokyo Yushun](/wiki/Tokyo_Yushun "Tokyo Yushun") (Japanese Derby) the horse was the 7th most favored to win. The horse ran in the middle of the pack, which covered the first 1000 meters at a slow pace of 1:01.6 and the first mile 1:41.6. As the pack entered the final stretch, the horse took the lead, running the last three furlongs at a record breaking 32.7 seconds, and held on to the lead across the finish line even as [Rose Kingdom](/wiki/Rose_Kingdom "Rose Kingdom") closed in. This marked the first Tokyo Yushun victory for both Uchida, trainer Hideaki Fujiwara, and owner Toyomitsu Hirai. ==
Later on in Autumn, the horse was entered in to the [Kobe Shimbun Hai](/wiki/Kobe_Shimbun_Hai "Kobe Shimbun Hai") with the [Kikka Sho](/wiki/Kikuka-sh%C5%8D "Kikuka-shō") in mind. However, the horse was beaten by the winner, Rose Kingdom, by a neck. The horse was still scheduled to run, but a few days before the race, the vet that checked the horse noticed symptoms of muscle pain and the plans to enter the horse in the race were scrapped at the last minute.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Later on, the horse was sent to run in the [Japan Cup](/wiki/Japan_Cup "Japan Cup") and [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") but finished unplaced in both races.[[5]](#cite_note-raceform-5)

### 2011: Four-year old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2011: Four-year old season")]

Eishin Flash started the 2011 season with the [Osaka Hai](/wiki/%C5%8Csaka_Hai "Ōsaka Hai"), but finished third behind [Hiruno d'Amour](/w/index.php?title=Hiruno_d%27Amour&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hiruno d'Amour (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%83%92%E3%83%AB%E3%83%8E%E3%83%80%E3%83%A0%E3%83%BC%E3%83%AB "ja:ヒルノダムール")]. Later on, the horse ran the Spring [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho"), but finished 2nd to Hiruno d'Amour again. Later at [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen"), [Katsumi Ando](/w/index.php?title=Katsumi_Ando&action=edit&redlink=1 "Katsumi Ando (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%AE%89%E8%97%A4%E5%8B%9D%E5%B7%B1 "ja:安藤勝己")] took over the role of jockey following another accident that left Uchida injured. At Takarazuka, the horse finished third behind [Earnestly](/w/index.php?title=Earnestly&action=edit&redlink=1 "Earnestly (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%82%A2%E3%83%BC%E3%83%8D%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC "ja:アーネストリー")].[[5]](#cite_note-raceform-5)

In Autumn, the horse was entered in to the Autumn Tenno Sho, with [Christophe Lemaire](/wiki/Christophe_Lemaire "Christophe Lemaire") as jockey, where he finished 6th. The horse was unplaced in Japan Cup, and at Arima Kinen the horse finished 2nd behind [Orfevre](/wiki/Orfevre "Orfevre").[[5]](#cite_note-raceform-5)

### 2012: Five-year old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2012: Five-year old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/2012_Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_%28Autumn%29_002.jpg/250px-2012_Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_%28Autumn%29_002.jpg)](/wiki/File:2012_Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Autumn)_002.jpg)

Demuro kneeling before the Emperor and Empress of Japan after winning the 2012 Autumn Tenno Sho

Eishin Flash was one of three Japanese horses that was entered in to [that year's Dubai World Cup](/wiki/2012_Dubai_World_Cup "2012 Dubai World Cup"), together with [Transcend](/w/index.php?title=Transcend_(horse)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Transcend (horse) (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%83%88%E3%83%A9%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%83%89_(%E7%AB%B6%E8%B5%B0%E9%A6%AC) "ja:トランセンド (競走馬)")] and [Smart Falcon](/wiki/Smart_Falcon "Smart Falcon"), where he finished 6th, which was the best of those three. After returning to Japan, he was entered in to the Takarazuka Kinen with Uchida returning as jockey, but finished 6th, with the 5th place horse beating him by 5 lengths. After a summer break, the horse started off unplaced in the [Mainichi Okan](/wiki/Mainichi_%C5%8Ckan "Mainichi Ōkan"), but at the Autumn Tenno Sho, which became the first Tenno Sho in 7 years to be attended by [Emperor](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan") [Akihito](/wiki/Akihito "Akihito") and [Empress Michiko](/wiki/Empress_Michiko "Empress Michiko"), the horse won his first race in 13 starts, and his second Grade I race since the Japanese Derby. Following the race, Eishin Flash's jockey, [Mirco Demuro](/wiki/Mirco_Demuro "Mirco Demuro"), dismounted from the horse, removed his helmet, and knelt in front of the Emperor and Empress.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

On the horse's final race of the season, the Arima Kinen, Demuro was diagnosed with [ureteral stones](/wiki/Ureter "Ureter") and [Kousei Miura](/wiki/Kousei_Miura "Kousei Miura") took over the role of jockey on the day of the race.[[9]](#cite_note-9) At the race, the horse briefly took over the lead briefly but was quickly overtaken by [Gold Ship](/wiki/Gold_Ship "Gold Ship") and ultimately finished fourth.[[5]](#cite_note-raceform-5)

### 2013: Six-year old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2013: Six-year old season")]

As Toyomitsu Hirai died on the March 2, ownership of the horse was transferred to Katsuhiko, his second born son. As for the horse himself, the horse started the season with the Osaka Hai on March 31, but finished 3rd behind Orfevre. After this, the horse was sent to Hong Kong to race the [Queen Elizabeth II Cup](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Cup "Queen Elizabeth II Cup") with Mirco Demuro, where he finished 3rd behind [Military Attack](/wiki/Military_Attack "Military Attack"), who finished a length and three-quarters in front of him. After a five and a half months long break, he was entered in to the Mainichi Okan and won. He was then entered to the Tenno Sho (Autumn) to win the race once again, but finished third. He later finished 10th in the Japan Cup. He was planned to run in the Arima Kinen as his final race, but his gait showed signs of problems, and as a result the horse was scratched. In spite of this, a retirement ceremony was still held at [Nakayama Racecourse](/wiki/Nakayama_Racecourse "Nakayama Racecourse") on December 23.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-:0-11)

## Stud career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Stud career")]

Following his retirement, he stood stud at [Shadai Stallion Station](/wiki/Shadai_Stallion_Station "Shadai Stallion Station"),[[11]](#cite_note-:0-11) before being transferred to Lex Stud in late 2018[[12]](#cite_note-12) where he stayed until his retirement from stud duties in late 2025. Now Eishin Flash resides at the Yogibo Versailles Farm in Hidaka.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eishin_Flash&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

An [anthropomorphized](/wiki/Moe_anthropomorphism "Moe anthropomorphism") version of Eishin Flash appears in the [Umamusume: Pretty Derby](/wiki/Umamusume:_Pretty_Derby "Umamusume: Pretty Derby") franchise, voiced by Ayami Fujino.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
